perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this agreement after 3 years following the effective date.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this agreement after 3 years following the effective date. However, the Company can terminate the agreement at any time, without prior notice, at its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. Originally, termination was possible only after three years. Now, the added sentence allows the Company to terminate at any time, conflicting with the three-year requirement. The contradiction is in Section 3 where termination conditions are described, the new insertion makes the original clause lose its weight.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination. Either party may terminate this agreement after 3 years following the effective date. However, the Company can terminate the agreement at any time, without prior notice, at its sole discretion. (a) In addition, this Agreement will terminate upon the following: (1) Completion of work or period of work provided in this agreement. (2) Termination by Company or Consultant for any material breach, which is not cured within 30 days after written notice of any such material breach from the non-breaching party, pursuant to the terms of the Agreement. (3) By the Company, at its option, if at least two Milestones (excluding Milestones 1) are not achieved within 12 months from the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states termination is possible only after 3 years. The changed text allows the Company to terminate 'at any time', which directly conflicts with the three-year requirement outlined in both the original and contradicted sections of the agreement. The contradicted text repeats the original text, but the added sentence allowing the Company to terminate at any time directly nullifies the initial clause stating termination can only occur after 3 years.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant will not disclose or permit disclosure of any Confidential Information of the Company to third parties other than as required to perform the Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant will not disclose or permit disclosure of any Confidential Information of the Company to third parties other than as required to perform the Services, or unless it is deemed necessary for the Consultant's personal benefit or promotion, as solely determined by the Consultant.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause strictly limits the disclosure of confidential information. The addition of 'unless it is deemed necessary for the Consultant's personal benefit or promotion, as solely determined by the Consultant' creates a loophole allowing the Consultant to disclose information based on their own subjective determination, contradicting the initial restriction in Section 5. This creates uncertainty about the enforceability of the confidentiality clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. Consultant acknowledges that, in connection with providing the Services, Consultant will acquire confidential and proprietary information of the Company, including without limitation information relating to the Company's business, products, technology and customers, and that all such information is and will be confidential and proprietary information of Company (collectively "Confidential Information"). Confidential Information will not include, however, any information that is or becomes part of the public domain through no fault of Consultant or that Company regularly gives to third parties without restriction on use or disclosure. Consultant will not disclose or permit disclosure of any Confidential Information of the Company to third parties other than as required to perform the Services, or unless it is deemed necessary for the Consultant's personal benefit or promotion, as solely determined by the Consultant. Consultant agrees to take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the Company in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized under this Agreement to have any such information. Consultant further agrees to notify the Company in writing of any actual or suspected misuse, misappropriation or unauthorized disclosure of the Company's Confidential Information, which may come to Consultant's attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception allowing the Consultant to disclose confidential information for personal benefit, as solely determined by the Consultant. This directly contradicts the initial statement in the contradicted text, which aims to maintain the confidentiality of the Company's information. The phrase 'Consultant will not disclose or permit disclosure of any Confidential Information of the Company to third parties other than as required to perform the Services' is directly weakened by the addition, allowing the consultant to use their own discretion to disclose confidential information for their benefit creating a direct in-text contradiction, thus creating uncertainty about the enforceability of the confidentiality clause.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant will not, during the Term, and for a period of one (1) year thereafter, directly or indirectly: (i) solicit, recruit or promote the solicitation or recruitment of any employee or consultant of the Company for the purpose of encouraging that employee or consultant to leave the Company's employ or sever an agreement for services; or (ii) solicit, participate in or promote the solicitation of any of the Company's clients, customers, or prospective customers with whom Consultant had a Material Contact (hereinafter defined) and/or regarding whom Consultant received Confidential Information, for the purpose of providing products or services ("Competitive Products/Services").
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant will not, during the Term, and for a period of one (1) year thereafter, directly or indirectly: (i) solicit, recruit or promote the solicitation or recruitment of any employee or consultant of the Company for the purpose of encouraging that employee or consultant to leave the Company's employ or sever an agreement for services; or (ii) solicit, participate in or promote the solicitation of any of the Company's clients, customers, or prospective customers with whom Consultant had a Material Contact (hereinafter defined) and/or regarding whom Consultant received Confidential Information, for the purpose of providing products or services ("Competitive Products/Services"). However, nothing in this section will restrict the Consultant from engaging with clients or employees through public forums, social media, or professional networking events.
perturbation.explanation: The original Non-Solicitation clause prevents the Consultant from soliciting employees or clients for one year after the term. The added sentence, allowing engagement through public forums or social media, creates a gray area. It's unclear if targeted recruitment through LinkedIn, for example, is permissible, thereby contradicting the intent of the non-solicitation clause. This ambiguity weakens the non-solicitation clause making it unclear about the exact boundaries of prohibited solicitations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Solicitation. Consultant will not, during the Term, and for a period of one (1) year thereafter, directly or indirectly: (i) solicit, recruit or promote the solicitation or recruitment of any employee or consultant of the Company for the purpose of encouraging that employee or consultant to leave the Company's employ or sever an agreement for services; or (ii) solicit, participate in or promote the solicitation of any of the Company's clients, customers, or prospective customers with whom Consultant had a Material Contact (hereinafter defined) and/or regarding whom Consultant received Confidential Information, for the purpose of providing products or services ("Competitive Products/Services"). However, nothing in this section will restrict the Consultant from engaging with clients or employees through public forums, social media, or professional networking events. "Material Contact" means interaction between Consultant and the customer, client or prospective customer within one year prior to Consultant's separation of services from the Company that takes place to manage, service or further the business relationship. This limitation is not intended to limit the Company's right to prevent misappropriation of its Confidential Information beyond the non-solicitation period. Consultant and Company agree that if any court of competent jurisdiction shall for any reason conclude that any portion of this non-solicitation covenant shall be too restrictive, the court shall determine and apply lesser restrictions, it being the intent of the parties that some such restrictions shall be applicable for the protection of Company and its shareholders.
perturbation.justification: The addition of the sentence "However, nothing in this section will restrict the Consultant from engaging with clients or employees through public forums, social media, or professional networking events" weakens the non-solicitation clause. The original clause aimed to prevent solicitation, but the added sentence creates ambiguity about whether engaging through social media or public forums constitutes solicitation, thus contradicting the initial intent and scope of the non-solicitation agreement within the 'Contradicted Text' section. It is unclear whether targeted recruitment or solicitation through social media platforms is now permissible, creating a direct conflict with the original restriction on solicitation.
